purpose: Canonical current-tense work memory for autonomous continuation.

current.goal: Harden the Python-first released-scope scaffold below pageReadyObserved so a lower-capacity agent can continue across consecutive one-commit slices.
current.milestone: Low-attention autonomous continuation remains unambiguous under the Python-first PRD contract
current.invariant: OpenClaw remains the only browser-facing actor, ez-ax remains orchestration-centric, the released ceiling remains pageReadyObserved, and canonical continuation memory remains two-tiered: work-rag for current state, rag for durable lessons.
current.next_action: Commit scaffold-hardening and Item 32 closure changes. Validate first: `python -m pytest -q tests/unit/test_transition_checkpoint_collection.py tests/unit/test_autoloop_prompt_driver.py` then `python -m mypy src/ez_ax/models/checkpoint.py src/ez_ax/models/transition.py tests/unit/test_transition_checkpoint_collection.py src/ez_ax/rag/autoloop_prompt_driver.py` then `python -m ruff check src/ez_ax/rag/autoloop_prompt_driver.py tests/unit/test_transition_checkpoint_collection.py`. If clean, stage and commit: `git add AGENTS.md docs/core-loop.md docs/current-state.md docs/llm/low-attention-coverage-ledger.json docs/llm/low-attention-execution-contract.json docs/product/rag.json docs/product/rag-archive.json docs/product/work-rag.json pyproject.toml src/ez_ax/rag/autoloop_prompt_driver.py tests/unit/test_transition_checkpoint_collection.py`. After commit succeeds, update work-rag current.next_action to the Queue-tail rewrite rule from docs/execution-model.md: rewrite work-rag current.next_action to one mandatory seeding-pass instruction for the active phase/milestone/anchor.

compression_policy.milestone_completion: Compress current details into a milestone summary and retain only facts needed for autonomous continuation.
compression_policy.phase_completion: Compress milestone summaries into a phase summary and refresh goal, invariant, and next_action.

history.milestone: Low-attention autonomous continuation remains unambiguous under the Python-first PRD contract
history.summary: Compressed 51 entries (48 same-scope milestone summaries + 3 other-scope historical entries covering Phase 0-4 and early R3). Items 1-31 of the low-attention queue closed across all released-scope families. TypeScript assets fully removed, Python-first PRD set established, LangGraph scaffold created, two-tier RAG initialized. Coverage ledger: 6 of 7 families covered; remaining family ('released evidence / reporting / comparability') awaits Item 32 (Transition Checkpoint Typing Contract). Autoloop prompt driver, slice-template routing, and all focused validation targets operational.

history.milestone: Low-attention autonomous continuation remains unambiguous under the Python-first PRD contract
history.summary: Promoted autonomous execution to a skill-first operator contract by adding a dedicated executable autoloop skill that wraps the validated runner, so users can invoke continuous implementation through a skill surface rather than a raw script command.

history.milestone: Low-attention autonomous continuation remains unambiguous under the Python-first PRD contract
history.summary: Reopened the low-attention queue after noticing that the active PRD truth is transport-agnostic while canonical current-state and queue wording still treated MCP as architectural truth; the next exact slice is now Item 27 to realign the injected OpenClaw boundary contract and demote MCP to an optional reference transport.
